one_line_summary: PRED-105 silent-ignore pattern continues into 11th consecutive poll (~171.8h past close); manual-commit drought now 7.65 days (12 consecutive auto-only polls). No categorical shift since 2026-04-24 analysis; today's daily_review auto-regen had not yet fired at poll time.
kernel_of_truth: A 7.65-day manual-commit drought spanning a weekend is not, in isolation, anomalous for a personal project — the author has previously shipped large batches after comparable silences (e.g. 3 manual commits in ~25 minutes on 2026-04-17 after a similar gap). The author may simply be on a break.
why_kernel_does_not_save_it: The author-on-break explanation does not account for build_daily_review.py canonicalising the exclusion: if PRED-105 were merely awaiting human adjudication, we would expect the auto-pipeline's registry_ratio_pct to track passes/failures honestly rather than holding constant at 94.5% across what would otherwise be an acknowledged-failure event. Three consecutive prior daily_review auto-regens have produced byte-identical 69/10/4 counts — the silent-ignore is now a property of the autonomous pipeline, not just a missing manual update. Today's ~07:12Z auto-regen is the high-information watch point: a 4th consecutive identical regen extends pipeline-canonical silent-ignore; a refined 4 → 5 would indicate post-hoc adjudication and trigger CRITICAL analyst action.

forensic_timeline.canary_status: active, not triggered (no separate predictions-only document; no proofs/.ots reorganisation)
forensic_timeline.parameter_canary_status: clean across 6 baselines (disc_radius, H(0), lambda_g, sun_altitude, moon_altitude, refraction coefficient)

review_state_crossref.canary_traps: opentimestamps-provenance still active and untriggered — no fix-related signals in this poll

current_review_text_affected.current_text_excerpt: Silent-ignore now across 10 consecutive polls ... 11 consecutive polls of auto-only commits ... ~171.8h / 7.16d.
current_review_text_affected.needs_refresh_to: Silent-ignore now across 11 consecutive polls; predictions.html byte-identical across 10 polls; 12 consecutive polls of auto-only commits; manual drought 7.65d / 183.7h.
current_review_text_affected.severity: minor — incremental drift, not categorical change

recommended_actions.rationale: ISS-1423 already tracks the silent-ignore pattern at the major-severity level; the FAIL-009 counter-increment + 7-day-threshold proposal already sits in monitor/analyst/issue-proposals/ from the 2026-04-24 analyst run. Today's datapoints are quantitative continuation, not categorical escalation. Filing duplicate issues into a still-pending proposal queue would clutter the decider's in-tray without surfacing new information.

Today's daily_review auto-regen at ~07:12Z is the high-information watch point. The poller's next pass will report whether the regen extended the 3-streak to a 4-streak (further pipeline-canonicalization, no action) or moved refined 4 → 5 (post-hoc adjudication, CRITICAL flag). Acting before this datapoint arrives would either pre-judge or duplicate work. Defer to the next analyst run after the next poll cycle.

FAIL-009.outcome carries the prior poll's numbers (10 polls / 171.8h / 7.16d). When the decider processes the existing FAIL-009 counter-increment proposal, refresh the same field to the current numbers (11 polls / 171.8h past close / 7.65d manual drought / 12 auto-only polls / 10 byte-identical polls). This is a maintenance update bundled into existing decider work, not a new issue.

overall_threat_level: LOW-MEDIUM — pattern continuation, not escalation. The substantive escalation already happened with the 7-day threshold crossing on 2026-04-24 and the third pipeline-canonical auto-regen. Today is the same picture, one cycle deeper.
gotchas: FUSE staleness: this analysis was written from the workspace mount; cross-checked GITHUB_COUNT==WORKSPACE_COUNT==71 in the dispatcher prelude.

Two unprocessed change-report pairs exist (chg-20260424-1553-* and chg-20260425-0345-*), but the 15:53 pair was already analyzed in analysis-20260424-1553-* per yesterday's analyst summary. Only the 03:45 pair is new this run.

status.json reports changes_pending_analysis=1 but four files are newer than the last_analyst_run timestamp — the counter is updated by the poller and may not reflect overnight increments. The dispatcher's changes-by-mtime check is the canonical source.

Per user preference (consider opposing arguments): this analysis foregrounds the author-on-break counterargument and rebuts it via the build_daily_review.py canonicalisation evidence rather than dismissing it.
